title: Apparatus and method for dynamic polygon or primitive sorting for improved culling
abstract: An apparatus and method are described for dynamic polygon or primitive sorting for improved culling. For example, one embodiment of an apparatus comprises: a rasterization unit to receive a plurality of polygons to be rasterized in an original ordering; and depth test evaluation logic to determine whether a current polygon is fully visible, partially visible or occluded; and reordering logic to incrementally alter the original ordering by swapping each occluded polygon with another polygon positioned relatively lower in the original ordering and by swapping each fully visible polygon with another polygon positioned relatively higher in the original ordering.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1 . An apparatus comprising: a rasterization unit to receive a plurality of primitives to be rasterized in an original ordering; and depth test evaluation logic to determine whether a current primitive is fully visible, partially visible or occluded; and reordering logic to incrementally alter the original ordering. 2. The apparatus as in claim 1 wherein the reordering logic is to incrementally alter the original ordering by swapping each occluded primitive with another primitive positioned relatively lower in the original ordering and by swapping each fully visible primitive with another primitive positioned relatively higher in the original ordering. 3. The apparatus as in claim 2 wherein each primitive comprises a specified number of polygons. 4. The apparatus as in claim 1 wherein primitives which are relatively higher in the original ordering are rasterized ahead of primitives which are relatively lower in the original ordering. 5. The apparatus as in claim 4 wherein the depth test evaluation logic determines whether a current primitive is fully visible, partially visible or occluded using depth information for each of the primitives collected from one or more prior rasterized frames. 6. The apparatus as in claim 2 wherein the rendering logic comprises a first pointer to identify a current primitive, a second pointer to identify a second primitive to be swapped with the current primitive if the current primitive is fully visible, and a third pointer to identify a third primitive to be swapped with the current primitive if the current primitive is occluded. 7. The apparatus as in claim 4 wherein the current primitive, the first primitive and the second primitive are identified using a vertex index list comprising pointers to the vertices of each of the primitives. 8. The apparatus as in claim 5 wherein the primitives comprise triangles and wherein each triangle includes three entries in the vertex index list identifying each of the three vertices of the triangle. 9. The apparatus as in claim 6 wherein upon swapping the current primitive, the first pointer is moved to the next primitive in the original ordering. 10. The apparatus as in claim 9 wherein upon swapping the current primitive with the second primitive, the second pointer is moved to the next primitive in sequence towards the bottom of the original ordering. 1 1 . The apparatus as in claim 10 wherein upon swapping the current primitive with the third primitive, the third pointer is moved to the next primitive in sequence towards the top of the original ordering. 12. The apparatus as in claim 2 wherein swapping the primitives comprises swapping entries associated with each of the primitives within a vertex index list comprising pointers to the vertices of each of the primitives. 13. The apparatus as in claim 5 wherein the reordering logic is configured to perform reordering of the partially visible primitives based on a detected amount of visibility of each of the partially visible primitives, wherein the partially visible primitives which have a relatively greater amount of visibility are positioned relatively higher in the ordering than partially visible primitives which have a relatively lower amount of visibility. 14. The apparatus as in claim 5 wherein a result of the reordering logic is a first bin of fully visible primitives ordered towards the top of the plurality of primitives, a second bin of partially visible primitives ordered in the middle of the plurality of primitives, and a third bin of occluded primitives, ordered towards the bottom of the plurality of primitives, wherein the reordering logic is configured to perform an additional 

 sorting within each bin, based on based on spatial data and/or depth data associated with each of the primitives. 15. The apparatus as in claim 14 wherein the spatial data comprises depth and/or color data and wherein the depth data comprises Z values associated with each primitive. 16. The apparatus as in claim 1 wherein each primitive comprises a single polygon. 17. The apparatus as in claim 16 wherein each polygon comprises a triangle. 18. A system comprising: a memory to store graphics program code and graphics data; one or more input/output (I/O) interfaces communicatively coupled to the memory and configured to receive graphics data from one or more I/O devices and store the received graphics data in the memory; a graphics processor to execute the graphics program code and responsively process the graphics data to render a plurality of image frames, the graphics processor further comprising: a rasterization unit to receive a plurality of primitives to be rasterized in an original ordering; and depth test evaluation logic to determine whether a current primitive is fully visible, partially visible or occluded; and reordering logic to incrementally alter the original ordering. 19. The system as in claim 18 wherein the reordering logic is to incrementally alter the original ordering by swapping each occluded primitive with another primitive positioned relatively lower in the original ordering and by swapping each fully visible primitive with another primitive positioned relatively higher in the original ordering. 20. The system as in claim 2 wherein each primitive comprises a specified number of polygons. 21 . The system as in claim 1 wherein primitives which are relatively higher in the original ordering are rasterized ahead of primitives which are relatively lower in the original ordering. 22. The system as in claim 21 wherein the depth test evaluation logic determines whether a current primitive is fully visible, partially visible or occluded using depth information for each of the primitives collected from one or more prior rasterized frames. 23. The system as in claim 2 wherein the rendering logic comprises a first pointer to identify a current primitive, a second pointer to identify a second primitive to be swapped with the current primitive if the current primitive is fully visible, and a third pointer to identify a third primitive to be swapped with the current primitive if the current primitive is occluded. 24. The system as in claim 21 wherein the current primitive, the first primitive and the second primitive are identified using a vertex index list comprising pointers to the vertices of each of the primitives. 25. The system as in claim 22 wherein the primitives comprise triangles and wherein each triangle includes three entries in the vertex index list identifying each of the three vertices of the triangle.
